ground_truth_fix: TL;DR. This is happening because the bpf_get_current_task helper doesn't preserve the type of the returned pointer. Instead, you'll want to use bpf_get_current_task_btf. Solutions There is fortunately an easy solution to this. You can use a newer BPF helper instead, bpf_get_current_task_btf which allows the verifier to preserve the type information via BTF.

candidate_repairs.patch_hint: Pass a trusted/BTF-typed object obtained from a verifier-recognized source. Casts from unrelated pointers will not satisfy this call.
candidate_repairs.rationale: The verifier sees R2 as a scalar value (scalar), but this call requires one of: a typed pointer, a trusted pointer, an RCU-protected pointer.

evidence.detail: rejected at provenance_tracing_programs.bpf.c:141: prov = bpf_task_storage_get(&provenance_structure_map_task, s_task, NULL, 0);
The verifier sees R2 as a scalar value (scalar), but this call requires one of: a typed pointer, a trusted pointer, an RCU-protected pointer.
Verifier symptom: R2 type=scalar expected=ptr_, trusted_ptr_, rcu_ptr_
No earlier dominating proof-establishing branch or narrowing was found.
